rationale: Review 1 correctly identifies (via its first two findings) that the PR builds specialized N/index-truncation results first and derives the Fintype/Fin cases from them via padding+attachFin+equiv, which is the wrong generality direction and forces mechanical boilerplate; acting on it yields a cleaner primary general statement with specials derived from it. Review 2's approval misses this material generality issue (confirmed directly in the diff's theorem order and reductions at lines 265/143/53).
